switching cost (UCB-Advantage)","value":"O(H|D_opt| log(T/(H|D_opt|)+1) + H|D_opt^c| log((H^4 S A^{1/2} log(SAT/δ))/(β sqrt(|D_opt^c|) Δ_min)) )"}],"novelty_claims":["First gap-dependent regret gap-dependent regret and policy switching cost bounds compared to previous works, especially in regimes with large action spaces or small suboptimality gaps."]}

methods: Error decomposition framework for gap-dependent regret analysis in Q-learning using reference-advantage decomposition and variance estimators
Gap-dependent analysis of UCB-Advantage algorithm
Gap-dependent analysis of Q-EarlySettled-Advantage algorithm
Martingale surrogate construction for reference-advantage decomposition in Q-learning
problems: Establishing gap-dependent regret bounds for Q-learning algorithms using variance estimators and reference-advantage decomposition
Establishing gap-dependent policy switching cost bounds for Q-learning algorithms
Improving upon worst-case regret and switching cost bounds for on-policy Q-learning in episodic tabular MDPs

results.value: O(((Q^* + β^2 H) H^3 S A log(SAT)) / Δ_min + (H^8 S^2 A log(SAT) log(T)) / β^2)
O(((Q^* + β^2 H) H^3 S A log(SAT)) / Δ_min + (H^7 S A log^2(SAT)) / β)
O(H|D_opt| log(T/(H|D_opt|)+1) + H|D_opt^c| log((H^4 S A^{1/2} log(SAT/δ))/(β sqrt(|D_opt^c|) Δ_min)) )

novelty_claims: First gap-dependent regret analysis for Q-learning algorithms using both variance estimators and reference-advantage decomposition for variance reduction.
First gap-dependent analysis of policy switching cost for Q-learning algorithms.

Development of a novel error decomposition framework that separates errors in reference estimations, advantage estimations, and reference settling, enabling gap-dependent analysis for Q-learning with variance estimators.
Introduction of martingale surrogate construction for handling non-martingale difficulties in reference-advantage decomposition in Q-learning.
Improved gap-dependent regret and policy switching cost bounds compared to previous works, especially in regimes with large action spaces or small suboptimality gaps.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["UCB-Advantage algorithm (a model-free reinforcement learning algorithm based on reference-advantage decomposition)"],"problems":["Achieving optimal regret bounds for model-free reinforcement learning in finite-horizon episodic Markov Decision Processes (MDPs)","Closing the gap between model-free and model-based RL algorithms in terms of learning efficiency (regret)","Ensuring low local switching cost in model-free RL algorithms","Applying model-free RL to concurrent reinforcement learning settings"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Regret","Local switching cost"],"results":[{"metric":"Regret","value":"\\tilde{O}(\\sqrt{H^2SAT})"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes UCB-Advantage, a novel model-free RL algorithm based on reference-advantage decomposition.","UCB-Advantage achieves a regret bound of \\tilde{O}(\\sqrt{H^2SAT}), matching the best known model-based algorithms and the information theoretic lower bound up to logarithmic factors.","First model-free algorithm to close the gap in regret bounds with model-based algorithms for finite-horizon episodic MDPs.","Demonstrates that model-free algorithms can be as sample-efficient as model-based algorithms in the tabular setting.","Shows that UCB-Advantage achieves low local switching cost and improves upon recent results in concurrent RL."]}

methods: UCB-Advantage algorithm (a model-free reinforcement learning algorithm based on reference-advantage decomposition)
problems: Achieving optimal regret bounds for model-free reinforcement learning in finite-horizon episodic Markov Decision Processes (MDPs)
Closing the gap between model-free and model-based RL algorithms in terms of learning efficiency (regret)
Ensuring low local switching cost in model-free RL algorithms
Applying model-free RL to concurrent reinforcement learning settings
novelty_claims: Proposes UCB-Advantage, a novel model-free RL algorithm based on reference-advantage decomposition.
UCB-Advantage achieves a regret bound of \tilde{O}(\sqrt{H^2SAT}), matching the best known model-based algorithms and the information theoretic lower bound up to logarithmic factors.
First model-free algorithm to close the gap in regret bounds with model-based algorithms for finite-horizon episodic MDPs.
Demonstrates that model-free algorithms can be as sample-efficient as model-based algorithms in the tabular setting.
Shows that UCB-Advantage achieves low local switching cost and improves upon recent results in concurrent RL.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Gap-dependent analysis of regret and communication cost for on-policy federated Q-learning","Novel framework leveraging suboptimality gap in tabular episodic finite-horizon MDPs"],"problems":["Obtaining tighter (gap-dependent) regret and communication cost bounds for federated Q-learning in tabular episodic finite-horizon MDPs","Overcoming the limitations of worst-case (gap-independent) analysis in federated reinforcement learning"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Regret bound","Communication cost bound","Global switching cost"],"results":[{"metric":"Regret bound","value":"O(log T) (gap-dependent), improving over previous O(sqrt(T)) bounds"},{"metric":"Communication cost bound","value":"Refined bound with log T term independent of MSA (number of agents, states, actions)"},{"metric":"Global switching cost (M=1)","value":"log T term independent of SA"}],"novelty_claims":["First gap-dependent analysis of regret and communication cost for on-policy federated Q-learning in tabular episodic finite-horizon MDPs","Framework achieves log T-type regret bound (gap-dependent), improving over previous sqrt(T)-type bounds","Communication cost bound disentangles exploration and exploitation and removes dependence on MSA from log T term","Gap-dependent communication cost bound yields better global switching cost for single-agent case (removes SA from log T term)"]}

methods: Gap-dependent analysis of regret and communication cost for on-policy federated Q-learning
Novel framework leveraging suboptimality gap in tabular episodic finite-horizon MDPs
problems: Obtaining tighter (gap-dependent) regret and communication cost bounds for federated Q-learning in tabular episodic finite-horizon MDPs

results.value: O(log T) (gap-dependent), improving over previous O(sqrt(T)) bounds

log T term independent of SA

novelty_claims: First gap-dependent analysis of regret and communication cost for on-policy federated Q-learning in tabular episodic finite-horizon MDPs
Framework achieves log T-type regret bound (gap-dependent), improving over previous sqrt(T)-type bounds
Communication cost bound disentangles exploration and exploitation and removes dependence on MSA from log T term

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ELEANOR-LowSwitching algorithm (Algorithm 1) using the doubling trick for low inherent Bellman error setting","Algorithm 2 using the doubling trick for generalized linear function approximation setting"],"problems":["Reinforcement learning with costly policy switching (minimizing switching cost)","Achieving near-optimal regret in RL with limited policy adaptivity","Extending low switching cost RL algorithms beyond linear MDPs to more general settings (linear Bellman-complete MDPs with low inherent Bellman error and generalized linear function approximation)"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Regret","Switching cost"],"results":[{"metric":"Switching cost (Low IBE setting, Algorithm 1)","value":"O(∑_{h=1}^H d_h log K)"},{"metric":"Regret (Low IBE setting, Algorithm 1)","value":"~O(∑_{h=1}^H d_h sqrt(K) + ∑_{h=1}^H sqrt{d_h} Z K)"},{"metric":"Switching cost lower bound (Low IBE, Z=0)","value":"Ω(∑_{h=1}^H d_h)"},{"metric":"Switching cost (GLM setting, Algorithm 2)","value":"O(d H log K)"},{"metric":"Regret (GLM setting, Algorithm 2)","value":"~O(H sqrt{d^3 K})"},{"metric":"Switching cost lower bound (GLM)","value":"Ω(d H)"}],"novelty_claims":["First algorithms to achieve near-optimal regret and logarithmic (in episodes) switching cost in linear Bellman-complete MDPs with low inherent Bellman error and in generalized linear function approximation settings, both of which generalize linear MDPs.","Prove matching lower bounds for switching cost in both settings, showing optimality up to logarithmic factors.","Demonstrate that the doubling trick can be leveraged for low switching cost RL in generalized linear function approximation, not just linear MDPs."]}

problems: Reinforcement learning with costly policy switching (minimizing switching cost)

results.metric: Switching cost lower bound (Low IBE, Z=0)

novelty_claims: First algorithms to achieve near-optimal regret and logarithmic (in episodes) switching cost in linear Bellman-complete MDPs with low inherent Bellman error and in generalized linear function approximation settings, both of which generalize linear MDPs.
Prove matching lower bounds for switching cost in both settings, showing optimality up to logarithmic factors.
Demonstrate that the doubling trick can be leveraged for low switching cost RL in generalized linear function approximation, not just linear MDPs.

methods: FedQ-Hoeffding: a federated Q-learning algorithm with Hoeffding-type concentration bounds

novelty_claims: First model-free federated reinforcement learning algorithms (FedQ-Hoeffding and FedQ-Bernstein) to achieve linear regret speedup with logarithmic communication cost in the number of time steps.
Introduction of an event-triggered synchronization mechanism for communication and policy switching in federated Q-learning.
Adaptive exploration policy selection for all clients to achieve linear regret speedup, in contrast to previous works with static policies.
Development of new concentration inequalities to handle non-martingale difference sequences arising from equal weight assignment in global aggregation.
Theoretical analysis showing that linear regret speedup and logarithmic communication cost are achievable in model-free federated RL.

novelty_claims: First analysis in the model-free setting (Q-learning) that establishes \( \sqrt{T} \) regret without requiring access to a simulator.
Q-learning with UCB exploration achieves regret matching the optimal model-based regret up to a single \( \sqrt{H} \) factor.
Demonstrates that model-free Q-learning can be made provably sample efficient in episodic MDPs with finite states and actions.
Introduces a crucial learning rate schedule (\( \alpha_t = O(H/t) \)) for Q-learning that is essential for achieving sample efficiency.

novelty_claims: Provides instance-dependent (local minimax) lower bounds for policy evaluation in discounted MDPs under a generative model, both asymptotic and non-asymptotic.
Shows that the widely-used TD algorithm is strictly suboptimal in the non-asymptotic, instance-dependent setting, even with Polyak-Ruppert averaging.
Introduces and analyzes variance-reduced forms of stochastic approximation that achieve non-asymptotic, instance-dependent optimality up to logarithmic factors.
Offers a theoretical framework for comparing policy evaluation algorithms based on instance-dependent guarantees, rather than worst-case analysis.
